reasoning: Query asks specifically for indoor Kagoshima attractions during a typhoon. Response returns general get_spots with outdoor-heavy results: Sakurajima (outdoor volcano), Yakushima (outdoor island), Jomon Sugi (outdoor). Only Kagoshima Castle has indoor_capable=mixed. No filtering for indoor_capable=indoor or mixed.
missing_data: Dedicated indoor venues: museums (e.g., Reimeikan Prefectural Museum), aquariums, covered shopping arcades. Slots with indoor_capable=indoor are absent. Typhoon safety advice missing.
improvement_hint: Filter or rank results by indoor_capable=indoor or mixed when query contains weather-emergency keywords (typhoon/storm). Surface museum-kind spots near top.

reasoning: Rainy Kyoto query needs indoor spots. Response returns general Kyoto spots: Nijo Castle (mixed), Toji Temple (mixed), Nagaoka-kyo (outdoor archaeological site). The outdoor items are not useful for rain. No filtering on indoor_capable. zh response language is correct.
missing_data: True indoor venues: Kyoto National Museum, Nishiki Market, indoor gallery spaces. Nagaoka-kyo outdoor archaeological site is a poor rainy-day pick.
improvement_hint: Apply indoor_capable filter (indoor or mixed) when weather constraints present. Deprioritize outdoor/archaeological sites in weather-constrained queries.

reasoning: Query asks for cool Nagano summer spots (highlands/caves). Response returns irrelevant Yamagata tourism portal pages twice as top results, then Shimane cool-down tag page. No Nagano cave or highland spots appear. STAY YAMAGATA is clearly wrong prefecture. Massive constraint failure.
missing_data: Nagano-specific highland/plateau spots (Norikura, Shirauma, Sugadaira), cave spots (Ryugashido in Iwate is nearby analogy but Nagano has Kakuenbochi caves), onsen retreats.
wrong_data: STAY YAMAGATA results (Yamagata, not Nagano) ranked top. Results are irrelevant geographically and thematically.
improvement_hint: search_hybrid needs prefecture filtering for this query type. Add prefecture=Nagano to hybrid search or post-filter by prefecture. Cross-prefecture noise dominates scores.

reasoning: Korean query for indoor Hokkaido spots during summer heat. Response returns general Hokkaido get_spots: Showa-Shinzan (outdoor volcano), Sapporo Clock Tower (indoor - good), Matsumae Castle (mixed). Showa-Shinzan as first result is outdoor and irrelevant. Indoor filtering not applied. Korean language names present.
missing_data: Indoor-capable Hokkaido attractions: Hokkaido Museum, Hokkaido Jingu interior, Sapporo Beer Museum, Hokkaido University Museum.
improvement_hint: Surface indoor_capable=indoor spots first when heat-relief or bad-weather constraints detected. Volcano/outdoor first position is counterproductive for this query.

reasoning: Rainy Kanazawa museum query. Hybrid search finds Kanazawa 金沢菓子木型美術館 and 国立工芸館 (relevant), but top BM25 result is a Mizunami city (Gifu) museum list page — wrong prefecture. Results are mixed and noisy. The two Kanazawa wikidata items are genuinely useful but buried.
missing_data: 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art Kanazawa (a top priority for this query), Kenroku-en (mixed indoor/outdoor), D.T. Suzuki Museum. These flagship indoor Kanazawa venues are absent.
wrong_data: Top ranked municipal_scrape spot is from Mizunami, Gifu — completely wrong location for Kanazawa query.
improvement_hint: Add Kanazawa/Ishikawa prefecture filter to hybrid search. 21st Century Museum is the canonical answer for rainy Kanazawa and must appear in top results.

reasoning: French query for covered Nara spots excluding deer. get_spots returns Yoshino (outdoor mountain), Takamatsuzuka Tomb (mixed), Kofukuji Temple (mixed), Koriyama Castle. No French descriptions. Outdoor mountain first. Deer-exclusion constraint not handled. A few mixed-capable spots are present but not filtered.
missing_data: Nara National Museum (definitive rainy day answer), Yakushiji temple interior, indoor cultural facilities. French descriptions absent despite lang=fr.
improvement_hint: Prioritize museum and indoor_capable=indoor/mixed spots. Generate French descriptions or flag missing translation. Deer-exclusion semantic constraint needs NLP routing.

reasoning: Okinawa heavy rain — aquarium/indoor request. Response returns Nakagusuku Castle (mixed), Tamaudun (mixed monument), Kumejima island (outdoor/nagisa), Zakimi Castle (mixed). No aquarium at all. Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium — the canonical answer — absent entirely.
missing_data: Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium is the top answer for this exact query type and is completely absent. DFS Ocean Expo Park, Ryukyu Mura indoor elements also missing.

reasoning: German-language query for Hiroshima indoor spots besides Peace Museum. Response returns Arataniyama (outdoor mountain) in English/Chinese/Traditional Chinese — not German, and purely outdoor scenic spot. Response fails on language (no German), fails on indoor constraint, and fails on relevance.
missing_data: German descriptions absent. Indoor Hiroshima spots: Hiroshima Museum of Art, Mazda Museum, indoor castle exhibits. Onomichi museums. Peace Memorial Museum itself should appear.
improvement_hint: German translation coverage needs improvement for Hiroshima municipal scrape. Must filter/rank indoor_capable spots for weather-constrained queries. Remove outdoor mountain as top result.

reasoning: Hokkaido October all-weather query. Hybrid search for 全天候 屋内 returns STAY YAMAGATA (Yamagata, completely wrong prefecture) twice as top results, then Kumamoto coastal content. No Hokkaido results. Complete constraint and geographic failure.
missing_data: Hokkaido indoor/all-weather spots: Sapporo Dome area, Hokkaido Museum, Asahikawa Science Center, indoor onsen facilities.

reasoning: Search_area for 洞窟 龍泉洞 returns Ryusenji-do (Kochi, different from queried) plus Ryusendo in Iwate (correct!) as second result, plus other cave types. Ryusendo (Q7385643) is the exact Iwate cave requested. Result is well-structured with heritage designations, coordinates, and municipality. Multi-prefecture cave results provide breadth.
missing_data: Visit info (opening hours, price) missing for Ryusendo. Additional Iwate cool-summer spots like Geibikei Gorge could be included.
improvement_hint: Surface practical visit metadata (hours/fee) for top cave results. Rank Iwate results higher when query specifies Iwate prefecture.

reasoning: Thai-language Fukuoka rainy-day museum query. Response returns Fukuoka Castle (mixed), Mizuki fortification (mixed outdoor), Mino Fault (no indoor), Dazaifu (outdoor). No actual museums. No Thai descriptions available. Indoor filtering not applied.
missing_data: Fukuoka Asian Art Museum, Hakata Machiya Folk Museum, Fukuoka City Museum — canonical rainy-day indoor answers. Thai language descriptions absent.

reasoning: Osaka August heat — air-conditioned attractions for full day. Response has Osaka Castle Museum description (good, indoor museum), Garden Oriental (venue/restaurant), Sumiyoshi Angū shrine (outdoor). missing_data: Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan, Osaka Museum of History, National Museum of Art Osaka — top indoor summer answers missing. Air-conditioning confirmation absent.

reasoning: Chinese typhoon Fukuoka indoor query. Same response as R-336 in Chinese: Fukuoka Castle (mixed), Mizuki (mixed), Mino Fault (outdoor), Dazaifu (outdoor). No museums. Typhoon-safety urgency not addressed. Chinese descriptions present via name fields only.

reasoning: Tottori/Shimane rainy-day museums query but only Tottori queried. Returns Tottori Castle (mixed), Utsubuki-Tamagawa preservation district (mixed outdoor), Yonago Castle (mixed), Tottori Sand Dunes (outdoor). No museums or art galleries. reasoning: Vietnamese rainy Sapporo query with municipality filter. Returns Sapporo Clock Tower (indoor), Sapporo Snow Festival (event), Hokkaido Univ historic buildings, Odori Park (outdoor but noted). Clock Tower is genuinely indoor and relevant. reasoning: Fukushima rainy day Japan Heritage query. Returns Oze (outdoor nature), Shiramizu Amida-do (temple — could visit in rain), National Treasure Buddhist items. reasoning: Korean rainy Nikko cultural/shrine indoor query. Returns Rinno-ji (mixed indoor temple), Cedar Avenue (outdoor but nominal), Nikko Tosho-gu (mixed, website+hours+fee), Futarasan Shrine. Municipality filter correctly applied. Korean names present. Opening hours and fees included. Shrine interiors genuinely partially indoor. Good relevance.

reasoning: Query asks about post-typhoon road closures and attraction operational status — a real-time information need. get_dmo returns DMO registry list (administrative org names and plan PDFs). This is completely wrong tool for this need. DMO data cannot answer whether roads are open or facilities operational. Complete retrieval failure for actual intent.
missing_data: Live road/facility status is fundamentally outside MCP scope (no live data). Should acknowledge limitation and direct to Tokushima DMO website/NHK disaster info. Spot data with contact info would at least enable direct inquiry.
improvement_hint: For post-disaster status queries, acknowledge live-data limitation explicitly. Return DMO contact info and official DMO website where real-time advisories may be posted.

reasoning: Query asks about Oku-Mikawa Hana Matsuri (花祭り) specifically. get_festivals returns Aichi intangible festivals: Kamezaki Shiohi Festival, Inuyama Festival, Chiryu Festival, Sunari Festival, plus hanabi events. 花祭り itself is NOT present — this Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property should be in corpus. Response gives adjacent Aichi festivals but misses the specific target.
missing_data: 花祭り (Hana Matsuri) of Oku-Mikawa — a nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property held in winter in Shitara area. This is the exact festival queried.
improvement_hint: Verify 花祭り (Q likely present in Wikidata) is in bunka_intangible corpus. If absent, add to Aichi bunka records. Query name matching should catch direct named festival queries.

reasoning: Tono folk legend kappa query. Hybrid returns とおの物語の館 (Tono Monogatari no Yakata — exact answer), 遠野観光モデルコース (tourist route), 遠野ふるさと商社 DMO plan. かっぱの夫婦像 from Yamagata appears but is noted as related to Tono exchange. missing_data: Kappa-buchi (河童淵) — the specific pool associated with kappa legends — not explicitly identified. Tono Furusato Village (伝承園) is mentioned in passing description but not as standalone result.

reasoning: Chinese query for hidden villages and hiking in Shirakami-Sanchi. search_area returns Shirakami-Sanchi UNESCO WHS correctly as top result, plus Akita Shirakami Tourism DMO plan with specific area details (Happo-cho, Nishimeya village content), and Shiraga-dake mountain. DMO chunk gives village-level detail. However Kumamoto content appears as noise.
missing_data: Nishimeya Village (Aomori) and specific trailheads for Shirakami hiking. Hidden village (廃村/秘境集落) content not present — query asks for abandoned/hidden villages specifically.
wrong_data: Kumamoto prefecture results (Amakusa coastal content) are irrelevant noise in BM25 results.
improvement_hint: Add Nishimeya and Fujisato (hidden-village type entries) if available. Filter out clearly off-topic prefectures in search_area for geographically specific UNESCO site queries.

reasoning: Query specifically about Kumejima tsumugi silk fabric — craftspeople and workshop visits. get_local_specialty returns Okinawa MAFF food GI items (moromi vinegar, peppers, carrots, chinsuko sweets). No crafts at all. 久米島紬 is a METI traditional craft, not MAFF food. Tool returns only food items for Okinawa, missing the craft entirely.
missing_data: 久米島紬 as METI traditional craft designation. Workshop/studio location on Kumejima island. Craftspeople and visit info.
improvement_hint: get_local_specialty should include METI densan (traditional crafts) records for Okinawa. 久米島紬 is designated 経済産業大臣指定伝統的工芸品 and should appear in craft results.

reasoning: Wajima lacquer decline and craftspeople query. get_local_specialty returns Ishikawa food items: sake brand, Kaga Maruimo yam, Noto korogaki persimmon, Ishiri/Ishiru fish sauce. No lacquerware. Wajima-nuri is a METI traditional craft and should appear but is completely absent from results.
missing_data: 輪島塗 (Wajima lacquerware) as METI traditional craft — the specific subject of the query. Workshop visit locations in Wajima. Whether post-2024 earthquake recovery affects craftspeople access.
improvement_hint: Ensure METI densan records include Wajima-nuri (輪島塗) for Ishikawa. This is Japan's most famous lacquerware and must appear when queried. Craft category filter should surface non-food items.

reasoning: Korean query for hidden/unknown waterfalls and gorges in Miyazaki. Returns Takachiho Gorge (known, not hidden), Hasugaike Kofun (not a waterfall), Kirishima Mountains (Kagoshima-adjacent, outdoor). Takachiho is the correct prefecture and relevant for gorges. However query asks for lesser-known sites and all returned spots are well-known. Korean names partially present.
missing_data: Lesser-known Miyazaki waterfalls: Nanatsuke Falls (七つ滝), Shiraito Falls, Kushima area coastal gorges. More obscure municipal scrape content for Miyazaki waterfall sites.
improvement_hint: Takachiho is correct but query asks for hidden/lesser-known. Lower-prominence scrape content for Miyazaki needs indexing. Semantic search for 隠れた滝 or 秘境 瀑布 would improve hidden-gem retrieval.

reasoning: French query for abandoned/ghost villages in Shimane. Response returns general Shimane spots: Lake Shinji (outdoor lake), Matsue Castle (standard tourist site), Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine (mining heritage — somewhat related to abandonment theme), Mount Sanbe. No abandoned village (廃村) content at all. Query requires specialized niche data not indexed.
